why: Protocol 775 Login clientbound minecraft:cookie_request must dispatch from the official framed packet id to a cookie_request key body decoder through Stevenarella packet_by_id. This is packet-support evidence only and is separate from cookie storage policy, cookie request/response runtime behavior, Login-to-Configuration state transition handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.
what: The Rust oracle test login_cookie_request_clientbound_framed_dispatch_matches_official_oracle_answer does not compile because Stevenarella has no Packet::LoginCookieRequest variant for the official Login clientbound cookie_request surface. The current v26_1_2.rs Login clientbound mapping also stops at minecraft:custom_query / 0x04 and does not map official packet id 0x05.

evidence.summary: Generated from official client.jar direct calls: Identifier.parse(String), ClientboundCookieRequestPacket(Identifier), ClientboundCookieRequestPacket.STREAM_CODEC, LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND.codec().encode/decode(ClientboundCookieRequestPacket), LoginProtocols.CLIENTBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets(...), and ClientboundCookieRequestPacket.key().
Manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and framed_dispatch_decode comparison surface.
Rust test reads the manifest and answer, splits official framed VarInt packet id from body bytes, and calls packet::packet_by_id for Login clientbound cookie_request dispatch.
Current Protocol 775 Login clientbound mapping contains login_disconnect 0x00, hello 0x01, login_finished 0x02, login_compression 0x03, and custom_query 0x04, but not cookie_request 0x05.

observed_failure: error[E0599]: no variant or associated item named `LoginCookieRequest` found for enum `Packet`; Login clientbound 0x05 is not mapped in v26_1_2.rs
